name: Three Villages Trail
name_original: GR-93, Barranco del Manzanar, Anguiano – ruta circular
description: Hike the difficult 10.4-mile GR-93 Barranco del Manzanar circular route in La Rioja, featuring riverine landscapes and waterfalls.
region_intro: Hiking around Anguiano offers diverse terrain, characterized by its location on the eastern slopes of the Sierra de Cameros and bordered by the Najerilla river. The region features imposing cliffs, varied mountainous environments, and dense forests. This topography provides a range of elevation changes and scenic riverine landscapes for hikers to explore. Best hiking trails around Anguiano The most popular hiking route is Three Villages Trail, a difficult 10.4 miles (16.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Monasterio de Valvanera loop from Monasterio de Valvanera, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) path. This trail offers easily accessible paths around the monastery, set in a natural environment. Local hikers also love the Anguiano – Anguiano Village loop from Anguiano, a 5.6 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through the village surroundings, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Anguiano is defined by mountainous terrain, riverine landscapes, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging long-distance trails. The routes in Anguiano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Anguiano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Anguiano?
A: Anguiano offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy loops to challenging long-distance routes through mountainous terrain and riverine landscapes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Anguiano?
A: Yes, Anguiano has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10463860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Valvanera loop from Monasterio de Valvanera&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.0 km path with accessible routes around the monastery, set in a beautiful natural environment.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Anguiano area?
A: Absolutely. Many trails around Anguiano are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17543838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguiano – Anguiano Village loop from Anguiano&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 9.1 km trail exploring the village surroundings.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while hiking near Anguiano?
A: Hikes around Anguiano offer stunning natural features including imposing cliffs, dense forests, and the Najerilla river. You can also discover the spectacular 18th-century Madre de Dios Bridge, which spans 30 meters above the river. The region is also known for its beautiful beech forests, especially vibrant in autumn.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls in the Anguiano region?
A: Yes, the region around Anguiano is known for its riverine landscapes, and some routes, like sections of the GR-93 Barranco del Manzanar circular route, feature waterfalls. These trails offer a chance to experience the dynamic natural beauty of the area.

Q: Is Anguiano a dog-friendly hiking destination?
A: While specific dog-friendly trail regulations can vary, the natural and open landscapes around Anguiano generally welcome dogs. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to follow local guidelines.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Anguiano?
A: The best time to hike in Anguiano is generally during spring, summer, and autumn. Autumn is particularly recommended for its vibrant colors in the beech forests. The varied terrain means some routes are enjoyable year-round, but always check local conditions, especially for higher elevations in winter.

Q: Are there any notable cultural or historical sites accessible from hiking trails?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10463860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Valvanera&lt;/a&gt; is a significant cultural site located about 15 km from Anguiano, set in a beautiful natural environment and serving as a starting point for hikes. Additionally, the UNESCO World Heritage San Millán de la Cogolla Monasteries (Suso and Yuso) are nearby and offer a blend of architectural and cultural heritage.

Q: Where can I park when starting a hike in Anguiano?
A: Anguiano, as a village, typically offers parking options within or on the outskirts of the village itself, which can serve as starting points for many local trails. For routes beginning at specific landmarks like the Monasterio de Valvanera, dedicated parking is usually available at the site.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails around Anguiano?
A: The trails in Anguiano are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from riverine landscapes to dense forests, and the variety of routes suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced hikers in Anguiano?
A: Experienced hikers will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989583387/three-villages-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Three Villages Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.7 km route with significant elevation gain, taking over 5 hours to complete. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17543833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Anguiano&lt;/a&gt;, a hard 14.6 km route with substantial elevation changes.

Q: Can I combine hiking with exploring nearby mountain passes or peaks?
A: Absolutely. The region around Anguiano, situated within the Sierra de Cameros, offers access to numerous mountain passes and peaks. You can explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Hincada Pass&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3536667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Rasa (1,420 m)&lt;/a&gt;, which provide amazing views and challenging climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on hikes around Anguiano?
A: Hikes around Anguiano feature a varied topography. You can expect mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes, riverine landscapes along the Najerilla river, and dense forests, including beautiful beech woods. The trails range from easily accessible paths to rugged mountain tracks.
tips: A beautiful walk, following the course of the river, crossing bridges all the time, and a small jump at the end. Easy, comfortable and pretty.

Las Cuevas is one of the three neighborhoods in the municipality of Anguiano. The Madre de Dios bridge (one of the most spectacular in La Rioja due to its steep enclave) separates it from the other two neighborhoods of the town.

Town with an illustrious past and spectacular beauty. It preserves noble houses with family coats of arms and stone is the protagonist of the old Madre de Dios bridge that rises over the Najerilla river.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing this circular route?
faqs.answer: This challenging hike typically takes around 5 hours and 15 minutes to complete. However, individual pace and stops for enjoying the scenery can affect the total time.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of accessible paths, but sure-footedness is required due to the varied terrain. You will follow the course of the Manzanar river, which involves crossing numerous bridges. Expect riverine landscapes and some sections that require good fitness.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Barranco del Manzanar trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided in the trail data, Anguiano Village is the starting point for this circular route. It's advisable to look for parking options within or on the outskirts of the village before beginning your hike.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this route?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify restrictions for dogs on this particular trail. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please check local regulations for any specific rules in La Rioja.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is described as a 'classic of the area' and can be enjoyed across seasons. However, given its 'difficult' rating and riverine nature, spring and autumn might offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Always check weather conditions before heading out, especially during winter or heavy rain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike this specific section of the GR-93 or the Barranco del Manzanar circular route. Most public hiking trails in Spain do not require permits, but it's always good practice to respect local regulations.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or features can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll encounter several interesting features. Keep an eye out for the beautiful Waterfall of the Manzanar Ravine. You'll also pass through the charming Las Cuevas neighborhood and Madre de Dios Bridge, and the hike starts and ends in the picturesque Anguiano Village itself.

faqs.question: Is this a family-friendly trail?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, 16.7 km distance, and 641 meters of elevation gain, this trail is generally not considered suitable for young children or families seeking an easy outing. It requires very good fitness and sure-footedness. Some user reviews mention sections being 'easy' or 'simple,' but this likely refers to specific portions, not the overall challenging nature of the full circular route.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is an integral part of the GR-93, a long-distance path traversing the southern part of La Rioja. It also connects to the GR-190 and the traditional pilgrimage route to the historic Monastery of Valvanera, offering opportunities for extended exploration if desired. Specifically, sections of the Sierras Riojanas and Ruta Tres Villas trails also overlap with this route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and length, you should come prepared. Essential items include sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water and snacks, appropriate clothing layers for changing weather, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A map or GPS device is also recommended, as is a hiking pole for stability on varied terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Anguiano?
faqs.answer: Anguiano Village, being the starting and end point of this circular route, is likely to offer amenities such as cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike. It's a traditional village, so you should find options for local cuisine and refreshments.

waypoints.name: Las Cuevas neighborhood and Madre de Dios Bridge
